Detect runway incursions or report failu.goal: Detect runway incursions or report failure
Detect runway incursions or report failu.strategy: To detect runway incursions or report failure, we can monitor the aircraft's position and speed, and check if it is within the runway's boundaries.

subgoals.G1.1: Monitor the aircraft's position and speed.
subgoals.G1.2: Check if the aircraft is within the runway's boundaries.

solutions.Sn1.1: Use radar or GPS to track the aircraft's position and speed.
solutions.Sn1.2: Use a geofence to define the runway's boundaries.

RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.goal: RSM data inadequacy detection is implemented with required dependability
RSM data inadequacy detection is impleme.strategy: The dependability of RSM data inadequacy detection can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The data processing algorithm is accurate.
subgoals.G1.3: The alarm system functions correctly.

Ownship runway locator reports failure w.goal: Ownship runway locator reports failure with p>=p2 if quality of information is inaccurate
Ownship runway locator reports failure w.strategy: The quality of information provided by the ownship runway locator can be determined by examining its sensors and data processing algorithms.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensors used by the ownship runway locator are accurate.
subgoals.G1.2: The data processing algorithms used by the ownship runway locator are accurate.

Conflict detector reports failure with p.goal: Conflict detector reports failure with p>=p3 if quality of information is inadequate
Conflict detector reports failure with p.strategy: The conflict detector reports failure if the quality of information is inadequate.

subgoals.G1.1: The quality of information is inadequate.

solutions.Sn1.1: The data sources are unreliable, the data is incomplete or inconsistent.

Traffic positions component reports fail.goal: Traffic positions component reports failure with p>=9 if quality of information is inadequate
Traffic positions component reports fail.strategy: The traffic positions component can be decomposed into its sub-components: sensor, processor, and transmitter.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor is able to detect failures.
subgoals.G1.2: The processor is able to process the data from the sensor.
subgoals.G1.3: The transmitter is able to transmit the data to the appropriate destination.

Traffic positions component correctly im.goal: Traffic positions component correctly implements reasonableness checks and error detection protocol
Traffic positions component correctly im.strategy: The traffic positions component can be broken down into its subcomponents: reasonableness checks and error detection protocol.

subgoals.G1.1: The reasonableness checks are correctly implemented.
subgoals.G1.2: The error detection protocol is correctly implemented.

UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.goal: UAVs flying in the managed airspace will not collide with fixed ground-based obstacles (FGB)
UAVs flying in the managed airspace will.strategy: The safety of UAVs flying in the managed airspace can be determined by examining their flight paths and the locations of fixed ground-based obstacles.

subgoals.G1.1: The flight paths of UAVs are known and predictable.
subgoals.G1.2: The locations of fixed ground-based obstacles are known and accurate.

solutions.Sn1.1: Flight plans, air traffic control data.

Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.goal: Comply to infrastructure safety case (ISAC) requirements.
Comply to infrastructure safety case (IS.strategy: The ISAC requirements can be decomposed into sub-requirements for each component of the infrastructure.

subgoals.G1.1: The hardware components comply with ISAC requirements.
subgoals.G1.2: The software components comply with ISAC requirements.
subgoals.G1.3: The human-machine interfaces comply with ISAC requirements.

UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.goal: UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based objects.
UAVs avoid all fixed Ground-Based object.strategy: UAVs can avoid all fixed Ground-Based objects by using a combination of sensors and algorithms.

subgoals.G1.1: The UAV is equipped with sensors that can detect fixed Ground-Based objects.
subgoals.G1.2: The UAV has algorithms that can process the sensor data and generate a path that avoids fixed Ground-Based objects.

Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.goal: Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) provides correct instructions for avoiding collisions between two UAVs.
Collision Avoidance Algorithm (CAA) prov.strategy: The correctness of the CAA can be determined by examining its components and their interactions.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system provides accurate information about the environment.
subgoals.G1.2: The path planning algorithm generates collision-free paths.
subgoals.G1.3: The control system can execute the planned paths accurately.

solutions.Sn1.3: Control system test reports, flight test data.

GPS is accurate within 5 cms..strategy: Accuracy within 5 cms means that the difference between the GPS-reported location and the true location is less than or equal to 5 cms 95% of the time.

subgoals.G1.1: The difference between the GPS-reported location and the true location is less than or equal to 5 cms 95% of the time.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test results from multiple locations and times.

The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.goal: The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery is sufficiently mitigated.
The hazard El1 -- Running out of battery.strategy: The hazard of running out of battery can be sufficiently mitigated by implementing a reliable charging system and a battery management system.

subgoals.G1.1: The charging system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The battery management system is reliable.

All identified causes for the hazard, El.goal: All identified causes for the hazard, El1, are sufficiently managed
All identified causes for the hazard, El.strategy: The causes for the hazard, El1, can be identified and managed.

subgoals.G1.1: The causes for the hazard, El1, are identified.
subgoals.G1.2: The causes for the hazard, El1, are managed.

The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.goal: The applied mitigations lead to sufficient risk reduction of the hazard El1
The applied mitigations lead to sufficie.strategy: The risk reduction of the hazard El1 can be evaluated by examining the effectiveness of the applied mitigations.

subgoals.G1.1: The applied mitigations are effective in reducing the likelihood of the hazard El1.
subgoals.G1.2: The applied mitigations are effective in reducing the severity of the hazard El1.

solutions.Sn1.1: Hazard analysis reports, risk assessment reports.

The mitigation requirement R1 holds.goal: The self-driving car is safe to operate on public roads.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The navigation algorithm is accurate.
subgoals.G1.3: The emergency systems function correctly.

Contract-based formal verification shows.goal: Contract-based formal verification shows with sufficient confidence that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover
Contract-based formal verification shows.strategy: The contract-based formal verification shows that R1 is achieved in the design of Inspection Rover.

subgoals.G1.1: The contract-based formal verification is correct.

System remains functional for intended r.goal: The self-driving car is safe to operate on public roads.
System remains functional for intended r.strategy: The safety of the self-driving car can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

subgoals.G1.1: The sensor system is reliable.
subgoals.G1.2: The navigation algorithm is accurate.

System and its elements are designed to.goal: System and its elements are designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission (NASA R&M).
System and its elements are designed to.strategy: The system and its elements can withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission if each of its components can withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission.

subgoals.G1.1: Each component of the system is designed to withstand nominal and extreme loads and stresses (radiation) for the life of the mission.

System is tolerant to radiation-induced.goal: System is tolerant to radiation-induced faults and failures (NASA R&M mod).
System is tolerant to radiation-induced.strategy: The system can tolerate radiation-induced faults and failures if its components are tolerant to radiation-induced faults and failures.

System is able to recover from anomalies.goal: System is able to recover from anomalies affecting functions that are important to top-level expectations (NASA R&M).
System is able to recover from anomalies.strategy: The system can recover from anomalies by having redundant components that can take over the functions of failed components.

subgoals.G1.1: The system has redundant components.
subgoals.G1.2: The redundant components can take over the functions of failed components.

COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.goal: COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requirement: No latch-up seen up to 5e9 protons/cm2 for 200 MeV protons.
COTS parts pass mission proton SEL requi.strategy: The COTS parts will pass the mission proton SEL requirement if they do not experience latch-up when exposed to 5e9 protons/cm2 of 200 MeV protons.

subgoals.G1.1: The COTS parts do not experience latch-up when exposed to 5e9 protons/cm2 of 200 MeV protons.

solutions.Sn1.1: Test results showing that the COTS parts did not experience latch-up when exposed to 5e9 protons/cm2 of 200 MeV protons.

We see a world where everyone who choose.goal: The self-driving car is safe to operate on public roads.

There is justified confidence that cyber.goal: There is justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
There is justified confidence that cyber.strategy: To show that there is justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services, we can break down the goal into sub-goals that address each of these aspects.

subgoals.G1.1: Cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety of the UK regulated services.
subgoals.G1.2: Cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the resilience of the UK regulated services.

solutions.Sn1.1: Risk assessments, security audits, penetration testing reports.
solutions.Sn1.2: Business continuity plans, disaster recovery procedures, incident response plans.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety of the UK regulated services.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the resilience of the UK regulated services.

solutions.Sn1.1: ARO's risk assessments, independent reviews of ARO's risk assessments.

Other stakeholders have justified confid.goal: Other stakeholders have justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services
Other stakeholders have justified confid.strategy: To show that other stakeholders have justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services, we can show that: 1) other stakeholders have confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services, and 2) this confidence is justified.

subgoals.G1.1: Other stakeholders have confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services.
subgoals.G1.2: This confidence is justified.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues do not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services at present
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: ARO's confidence in the safety and resilience of UK regulated services can be justified by examining the effectiveness of its cyber-security measures.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO has implemented effective cyber-security measures.
subgoals.G1.2: The cyber-security measures are effective in mitigating the risks to the safety and resilience of UK regulated services.

solutions.Sn1.1: ARO's cyber-security policies and procedures, evidence of compliance with relevant standards and regulations.
solutions.Sn1.2: ARO's cyber-security risk assessments, incident response plans, and evidence of successful implementation.

ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.goal: ARO has justified confidence that cyber-security issues will not pose unacceptable risks to the safety and resilience of the UK regulated services in the future
ARO has justified confidence that cyber-.strategy: ARO's justified confidence can be demonstrated by examining the effectiveness of its cyber-security measures.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO has implemented effective cyber-security measures.
subgoals.G1.2: ARO's cyber-security measures are aligned with industry best practices.
subgoals.G1.3: ARO's cyber-security measures have been independently verified.

solutions.Sn1.1: Documentation of cyber-security measures, results of security audits.
solutions.Sn1.2: Comparison of ARO's cyber-security measures with industry standards and best practices.

ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.goal: ARO regulatory activity provides justified confidence in the security and reslience
ARO regulatory activity provides justifi.strategy: ARO regulatory activity provides justified confidence in the security and reslience of the system.

subgoals.G1.1: ARO regulatory activity is effective in ensuring the security of the system.
subgoals.G1.2: ARO regulatory activity is effective in ensuring the reslience of the system.

solutions.Sn1.1: ARO regulatory activity reports, third-party assessments.
solutions.Sn1.2: ARO regulatory activity reports, third-party assessments.

ARO has justified confidence in the over.goal: ARO has justified confidence in the overall regulatory institutions and standards
ARO has justified confidence in the over.strategy: ARO's confidence in the overall regulatory institutions and standards can be decomposed into confidence in the institutions and confidence in the standards.

subgoals.G1.2: ARO has justified confidence in the regulatory standards.

ARO has justified confidence that the re.strategy: ARO's confidence in the regulated system's ability to deal with cyber related events and changes can be justified by examining the system's security controls and incident response procedures.

Sources of doubt actively managed - actu.goal: Sources of doubt actively managed - actual and perceived risks

The vehicle is acceptably safe.strategy: The safety of the vehicle can be determined by examining its hardware and software components.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements during product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument over the implementation of safety requirements during product development can be broken down into the following sub-goals: 1. The safety requirements are clearly defined and understood by all stakeholders. 2. The safety requirements are feasible to implement within the project's constraints. 3. The safety requirements are implemented correctly in the product.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined and understood by all stakeholders.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are feasible to implement within the project's constraints.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are implemented correctly in the product.

Argument over the implementation of safe.goal: Argument over the implementation of safety requirements post product development
Argument over the implementation of safe.strategy: The argument over the implementation of safety requirements post product development can be broken down into the following sub-goals: 1. The safety requirements are clearly defined and understood by all stakeholders. 2. The safety requirements are feasible to implement. 3. The safety requirements are cost-effective to implement. 4. The safety requirements do not adversely affect the product's performance or usability.

subgoals.G1.1: The safety requirements are clearly defined and understood by all stakeholders.
subgoals.G1.2: The safety requirements are feasible to implement.
subgoals.G1.3: The safety requirements are cost-effective to implement.
subgoals.G1.4: The safety requirements do not adversely affect the product's performance or usability.

The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.goal: The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Safety Requirements
The vehicle satisfies its Pre-defined Sa.strategy: The vehicle satisfies its pre-defined safety requirements if each of its sub-systems satisfies its pre-defined safety requirements.

subgoals.G1.1: The braking system satisfies its pre-defined safety requirements.
subgoals.G1.2: The steering system satisfies its pre-defined safety requirements.
subgoals.G1.3: The suspension system satisfies its pre-defined safety requirements.

System is acceptably safe to operate in.goal: System is acceptably safe to operate in the specified environment
System is acceptably safe to operate in.strategy: The system is acceptably safe to operate in the specified environment if it meets all applicable safety standards and regulations.

subgoals.G1.1: The system meets all applicable safety standards.
subgoals.G1.2: The system meets all applicable safety regulations.

solutions.Sn1.2: List of applicable safety regulations and compliance reports.

The residual risk associated with the id.strategy: The residual risk associated with the identified system hazards can be reduced to an acceptable level by implementing appropriate risk mitigation measures.

Random Failures: The impact of random fa.goal: Random Failures: The impact of random failures which can invalidate the Safety Goal U have been adequately managed
Random Failures: The impact of random fa.strategy: The impact of random failures can be managed by identifying potential failure modes, assessing their likelihood and severity, and implementing mitigations to reduce the risk.
